a11y.resetDefaultsTitle: Reset all accessibility settings to defaults
a11y.title: Accessibility
about.attributionsIntro: The Library is built on the shoulders of excellent open-source tools and libraries:
about.creationP1: The Library was conceived and designed by Bosco, who envisioned a self-hosted audiobook platform that treats a personal collection with the care and presentation it deserves — encrypted authentication, position syncing across devices, and an interface that makes browsing a pleasure rather than a chore.
about.creationP2: Development is a collaborative effort between Bosco and Claude (by Anthropic). From architecture decisions and security hardening to the Art Deco visual language and audio playback engine, every feature has been shaped through genuine partnership — human vision guiding AI capability, each strengthening the other.
about.deeplDesc: Neural machine translation for book descriptions, UI strings, and on-demand text translation — consistently producing the most natural Chinese renderings of English prose we tested.
about.ffmpegDesc: Audio conversion and metadata extraction. The backbone of the audiobook processing pipeline, handling format transcoding, chapter detection, and cover art extraction.
about.fido2Desc: Passkey authentication support enabling passwordless sign-in with hardware security keys, biometrics, and platform authenticators.
about.flaskDesc: Python web framework powering the API server and back-office administration interface. Lightweight, flexible, and battle-tested.
about.hfDesc: Home to the open model ecosystem that makes projects like this possible. The Library pulls Whisper, XTTS, and supporting models from the Hugging Face Hub — a public good maintained by a community that believes in open AI.
about.howlerDesc: Audio playback engine providing reliable cross-browser audio with Web Audio API fallback, volume control, and seeking support.
about.localizationIntro: Chinese (and other future language) support in The Library would not be possible without the generosity and craft of the open AI community and the GPU hosts who keep these models affordable to run. The Library extends heartfelt gratitude to:
about.localizationScope: Every element of the library that patrons interact with is fully translated — navigation, player controls, tooltips, book descriptions, subtitles, help documentation, and audio narration. The administrative backoffice is intentionally excluded from translation: it is used solely by the system operator and keeping it in English avoids doubling maintenance work for pages no patron ever sees.
about.mutagenDesc: Audio metadata parsing library for reading and writing tags across multiple audio formats including Opus, M4B, MP3, and FLAC.
about.pyotpDesc: TOTP-based authentication providing time-based one-time passwords compatible with standard authenticator apps.
about.runpodDesc: Serverless GPU platform used as a secondary provider for longer narration jobs. Fast spin-up times and fair pricing keep The Library's translated audio pipeline responsive.
about.sqlcipherDesc: Encrypted SQLite database providing transparent 256-bit AES encryption for user authentication data, session tokens, and account information.
about.subtitle: A Private Audiobook Collection
about.theLibraryP1: The Library is a private audiobook management system for organizing, converting, and streaming a personal audiobook collection. It provides a browser-based interface for browsing, searching, and listening to audiobooks from any device on the network.
about.theLibraryP2: Built with an Art Deco aesthetic inspired by the grand reading rooms of the 1920s, The Library combines modern web audio technology with classic design sensibilities — because a good book deserves a beautiful shelf.
about.title: About The Library
about.title.github: View the source code on GitHub
about.title.help: View the user guide and help documentation
about.vastDesc: Peer-to-peer GPU marketplace that runs Whisper transcription and XTTS narration on demand. Affordable cold-start GPU time is what makes per-book translation economically viable for a private library.
about.whisperDesc: Open-source speech-to-text model used to transcribe audiobook audio into source subtitles before translation. Accurate across accents, narration styles, and long-form listening.
about.xttsDesc: Multilingual voice cloning and text-to-speech synthesis, enabling translated narration that preserves the character of the original reader across languages.

claim.method.compareFido2: Physical Key: A small device you plug in or tap. Best if you already own one (like a YubiKey).

claim.method.comparePasskey: Face / Fingerprint / PIN: Easiest daily use. Sign in the way you unlock your phone. Best for most people.
claim.method.compareTotp: Free Phone App: Type a 6-digit number from your phone each time. Works everywhere.
claim.method.notSureBody: If you have a modern phone or computer, Use Your Face, Fingerprint, or PIN is the easiest choice. If you just want an email link each time, choose Sign In via Email.

email.activation.heading: Welcome to The Library
email.activation.how: How it works: Each time you want to sign in, enter your username and we'll email you a sign-in link. No passwords or apps needed!
email.activation.intro: You've been invited to The Library — a private audiobook collection. Click the big gold button below to activate your account and start listening.
email.activation.subject: Welcome to The Library
email.activation.text:
Welcome to The Library

Hello {username},

You've been invited to The Library — a private audiobook collection.

Click the link below to activate your account and start listening:

{activation_url}

This link works for {expires_hours} hours. After that, ask the admin to resend your invitation.

How it works: Each time you want to sign in, enter your username and we'll email you a sign-in link. No passwords or apps needed!
email.approval.fallback: If the button doesn't work, copy this link and paste it into your browser:
email.approval.heading: Welcome to The Library!
email.approval.instructions: You'll need an authenticator app on your phone (Google Authenticator, Aegis, or FreeOTP all work and are free). Then click the button below to set up your account with the claim token you saved when you requested access.
email.approval.intro: Great news — your access request has been approved! Follow the instructions below to finish setting up your account.
email.approval.subject: You're Approved! Welcome to The Library
email.approval.text:
Welcome to The Library!

Hello {username},

Great news — your access request has been approved! Follow these steps to finish setting up your account:

1. Install a free authenticator app on your phone (Google Authenticator, Aegis, or FreeOTP)
2. Find your claim token — the code you saved when you requested access
3. Open this link: {claim_url}
4. Enter your username ({username}) and claim token
5. Scan the QR code shown on screen with your authenticator app
6. Write down the backup codes shown — keep them somewhere safe
7. Type the 6-digit number from your app to finish!

If you can't find your claim token, contact the person who runs The Library.
email.common.heading: The Library
email.denial.body: We've reviewed your access request, and unfortunately we're unable to approve it at this time.
email.denial.retry: If you believe this was in error, you may submit a new request.
email.denial.subject: Update on Your Access Request — The Library
email.denial.text:
Hello {username},

We've reviewed your access request, and unfortunately we're unable to approve it at this time.

Reason: {reason}

If you believe this was in error, you may submit a new request.
email.invitation.fallback: If the button doesn't work, copy this link and paste it into your browser:
email.invitation.heading: Welcome to The Library!
email.invitation.instructions: Install a free authenticator app on your phone (Google Authenticator, Aegis, or FreeOTP), then click the button below to set up your account using the claim token above.
email.invitation.intro: You've been invited to The Library — a private audiobook collection. Follow the instructions below to set up your account.
email.invitation.subject: You're Invited to The Library!
email.invitation.text:
Welcome to The Library!

Hello {username},

You've been invited to The Library — a private audiobook collection.

=== YOUR CLAIM TOKEN (WRITE THIS DOWN!) ===
{claim_token}
============================================

Save this token! You'll need it to finish setting up your account. This invitation expires in {expires_hours} hours.

Steps to set up your account:
1. Install a free authenticator app on your phone (Google Authenticator, Aegis, or FreeOTP)
2. Open this link: {claim_url}
3. Enter your username: {username}
4. Enter the claim token shown above
5. Scan the QR code with your authenticator app
6. Write down the backup codes shown — keep them somewhere safe
7. Type the 6-digit number from your app to finish!
email.invitation.warning: Write this token down or save this email! You'll need it to finish setting up your account. This invitation expires in {expires_hours} hours.
email.magic_link.expiry: This link works for {expires_minutes} minutes. After that, you'll need to request a new one. If you didn't ask for this link, you can simply ignore this email.
email.magic_link.fallback: If the button doesn't work, copy the link below and paste it into your browser's address bar:
email.magic_link.intro: Click the big gold button below to sign in. That's it!
email.magic_link.subject: Sign In to The Library
email.magic_link.text:
Hello {username},

Click the link below to sign in to The Library:

{link}

This link works for {expires_minutes} minutes. After that, you'll need to request a new one.

If the link doesn't work, copy it and paste it into your browser's address bar.

If you didn't ask for this link, you can ignore this email.
email.reply.footer: This is a reply to your message to The Library.
email.reply.subject: Reply from The Library
email.reply.text:
Hello {username},

{reply_text}

---
This is a reply to your message to The Library.

register.claim.help.saveCopy: Copy it to a notes app on your phone
register.claim.help.saveScreenshot: Take a screenshot of this screen
register.claim.help.whatBody: This is your personal code that proves you're the one who requested this account. You'll need it to finish setting up after approval.
register.claim.title: Your Claim Token
register.claim.warning: Save this token! You'll need it to complete setup after your request is approved. This token will not be shown again.
register.complete.body: Your account is ready. You can now sign in with your username and authenticator code.
register.complete.title: Welcome to The Library
register.description: Access to The Library is by invitation only. Enter your desired username to request access. An administrator will review your request.
register.emailHelp.needBody: No. This is completely optional. You can complete registration without providing any personal information.
register.emailHelp.needTitle: Do I need to provide an email?
register.emailHelp.privacyBody: If you provide an email address, the administrator will have access to it. Only provide an email if you're comfortable with that.
register.emailHelp.skipBody: No problem! Just save your claim token (shown on the next screen) and check back later. You can complete setup using that token after approval.
register.emailHelp.skipTitle: What if I don't provide one?
register.emailHelp.whyBody: If you do, we'll send you an email when your request is approved, with helpful instructions for setting up your account.
register.emailHint: We'll email you when your request is reviewed
register.js.emailOrCheck: You'll receive an email when approved, or check
register.js.passkeySuccess: Your passkey has been set up successfully! Save your backup codes below.
register.js.securityKeyDesc: Use a security key (YubiKey, etc.) to sign in securely.
register.js.usernameMinLength: Username must be at least 3 characters
register.nextSteps.step1: An administrator will review your request
register.nextSteps.step3: Once approved, use your claim token to get your login credentials
register.nextSteps.step4: Set up your authenticator app and start using The Library!
register.nextSteps.title: What happens next?
register.pending.body: Your request for access has been submitted and is awaiting administrator review.
register.pending.emailInfo: We'll also send you an email when your request is reviewed.
register.pending.title: Request Submitted
register.recoveryEmail.help.recommendBod: Adding an email gives you a safety net. If something goes wrong, you won't be locked out permanently.
register.recoveryEmail.help.skipBody: You can still use backup codes (you'll get those in the next step). But having an email is an extra layer of protection.
register.recoveryEmail.help.skipTitle: What if I don't add one?
register.recoveryEmail.help.whatBody: If you ever lose your phone or can't get into your account, we can send a special link to this email to help you get back in.
register.recoveryEmail.hint: For account recovery via magic link
register.recoveryEmail.label: Recovery Email (optional)
register.title: Request Access
register.totp.help.app1password: 1Password - If you already use it for passwords
register.totp.help.app2fas: 2FAS - Free, open source, works on iPhone, Android, and browsers (2fas.com)
register.totp.help.appGoogle: Google Authenticator - Simple and free (search "Google Authenticator" in your app store)
register.totp.help.setup1: Download one of those apps from your phone's app store
register.totp.help.setup2: Open the app and look for "Add" or a "+" button
register.totp.help.setup3: Point your phone's camera at the square code below
register.totp.help.setup4: The app will beep and add "The Library" to your list
register.totp.help.setupTitle: How do I set it up?
register.totp.help.whatBody: It's a free app on your phone that creates a special 6-digit code. This code changes every 30 seconds, and only your phone knows what the next code will be.
register.totp.help.whyBody: Even if someone learns your username, they can't get in without your phone. It's like having a lock that changes every 30 seconds!
register.totp.qrHelp.cantScanBody: If the scan doesn't work, you can type the secret code shown below into your app instead. Look for "Enter code manually" or "Enter setup key" in your app.
register.totp.qrHelp.step1: Open your authenticator app on your phone
register.totp.qrHelp.step4: Point your phone's camera at this square pattern
register.totp.qrHelp.step5: Your phone will automatically read it and add The Library
register.totp.qrHelp.title: How to scan this code
register.totp.scanPrompt: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app:
register.totp.title: Set Up Authenticator
register.usernameHelp.body: A username is your personal name for this website. Think of it like a nickname that only you use here.
register.usernameHelp.pick2: It can be your name, a nickname, or anything you like
register.usernameHelp.pick3: It must be 3-24 letters or numbers
register.usernameHelp.title: What is a username?
register.verify.help.filledBody: If you clicked a link the administrator sent you, the code is already entered. You're all set!
register.verify.help.filledTitle: What if it's already filled in?
register.verify.help.whatBody: This is a special code that proves the library administrator approved your access request.
register.verify.help.whereBody: The administrator will send you this code. It might come:
register.verify.intro: Enter the verification code sent to you to complete your registration.
streaming.bilingual.title: Bilingual transcript
subtitleGen.offer: Translated subtitles aren't available for this book yet. Generate them now?
subtitleGen.phase.error: Subtitle generation failed. Please try again in a few minutes.
subtitleGen.phase.gpu_spinup: Waking up the GPU server (cold start can take a minute or two)…
subtitleGen.phase.transcribing: Transcribing audio — this can take several minutes…
ttsGen.offer: Translated audio isn't available for this book yet. Generate it now?
ttsGen.phase.error: Audio generation failed. Please try again in a few minutes.
ttsGen.phase.gpu_spinup: Waking up the GPU server (cold start can take a minute or two)…
ttsGen.phase.synthesizing: Synthesizing audio — this can take several minutes…
verify.checking: Checking your link... just a moment.
verify.errorDefault: This can happen if the link is more than 15 minutes old, you already used this link, or the link was copied incorrectly.
verify.help.checkSpam: Check your spam or junk folder
verify.help.waitAndCheck: Wait a few minutes and check again
verify.help.whereIsCodeBody: Check your email for a message from The Library. Copy the code from that email and paste it here.
verify.js.activated: Your account is activated. Taking you to The Library now...
verify.js.firewallBlocked: Request was blocked by the firewall. Please try again or contact admin.
verify.js.newLinkSent: Check your email! We sent you a new sign-in link. Click it to sign in.
verify.js.noServer: We couldn't reach the server. Please check your internet connection and try again.
verify.js.sendFailed: Unable to send a new link. Try signing in from the login page.
verify.manualDescription: If you received a code instead of a clickable link, paste it here.
verify.resendPrompt: Enter your username below and we'll send you a fresh link.
verify.subtitle: Checking Your Link
verify.successMessage: Taking you to The Library now...
webauthn.js.authCancelled: Authentication was cancelled or timed out
webauthn.js.regCancelled: Registration was cancelled or timed out
